title: LVIS Plugin Manifest
description: Validates plugin.json manifest files for LVIS plugins (§9)

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL identifying the manifest schema version this plugin targets.

id.description: Plugin unique identifier. Kebab-case (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens). Min 3 chars — 2-char names reserved for future system namespaces. Example: "my-plugin".

description.description: Short human-readable summary used in the inactive-plugin catalog LLM sees in the system prompt.

version.description: Stable SemVer (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, no leading zeros). Pre-release / build-metadata suffixes are not supported on the marketplace publish channel — the per-plugin publish.yml rejects them at tag time. Aligning the schema with that gate so AJV fails earlier.

tools.description: LLM- and app-visible tool surface: an array of pure MCP Tool objects (manifest == wire, #885). Each tool's `_meta.ui.visibility` declares model and/or app reachability. An empty `[]` is valid for template / MCP-server manifests that expose no plugin-authored tools.

window.description: Optional detachable-window defaults for this UI extension. When present the host creates a detached BrowserWindow for this view using these dimensions / snap hints.

width.description: Initial window width in DIP pixels

height.description: Initial window height in DIP pixels

ui.description: UI extensions. Kind-specific required fields (embedded-module: entry+exportName; embedded-page: page) are enforced at runtime with hard-fail — invalid entries fail manifest load, the plugin does NOT register.

uiResources.description: MCP App ui:// resources this plugin serves (distinct from `ui[]`, which declares host-mounted React sidebar panels). DECLARED POLICY, SERVED CONTENT: each entry declares a `ui://<pluginId>/<path>` uri plus that resource's OWN _meta.ui security policy (csp/permissions); the card HTML itself is served by the plugin at read time (`RuntimePlugin.readUiResource(uri)`), exactly as an external MCP server answers resources/read with bytes — the host never reads a plugin-declared disk path. When a tool result carries `_meta.ui.resourceUri` matching one of these uris, the loopback host asks the plugin for the HTML and renders it through the same sandbox-proxy + main-computed CSP path as an external MCP server's ui:// resource. Security (enforced fail-closed at serve time): the uri authority MUST equal the plugin id (own-namespace-only), the uri MUST be declared here (declared-only — this is what binds served content to a reviewed policy), and main COMPUTES the CSP header AND the frame's Permissions-Policy `allow` attribute from csp/permissions — the plugin never supplies a policy header string or an allow-list. csp/permissions stay in the manifest (not on the hook's return) so they are statically reviewable and covered by manifestSha256. See mcpUiResourcePermissions for the accepted permission set (host-honorable, proven end-to-end).

uri.description: ui://<pluginId>/<path>. Authority MUST equal the declaring plugin's id (re-checked fail-closed at serve time).

skills.description: Plugin-owned Skill directories. Each path is relative to the verified plugin root and must contain SKILL.md.

hooks.description: Plugin-owned declarative Hook configuration files. Declaration does not grant execution trust.

mcpServers.description: Plugin-owned declarative MCP server configuration files. Declaration does not grant connection approval.

capabilities.description: Free-form capability declarations (format-validated, NOT a closed enum). The host ENFORCES only two at runtime — 'external-auth-consumer' and 'host:overlay' (see src/plugins/capabilities.ts KNOWN_CAPABILITY_IDS). Every other string is a harmless self-declaration (e.g. the live 'worker-client' host discovery key, or legacy strings on already-installed manifests). Kept free-form — rather than an enum — so an installed manifest declaring a removed/legacy capability still validates and loads instead of being rejected at schema validation. Emit authorization for event-source namespaces (email/calendar/meeting/index) is inferred from emittedEvents, not from a capability here. The pattern is format hygiene only (blocks uppercase/whitespace/garbage), not a vocabulary.

networkAccess.description: Tier A host-mediated egress allow-list. hostApi.hostFetch may only reach hosts matching allowedDomains (dot-boundary suffix match). Deny-by-default: absent or empty = no egress.

allowedDomains.description: Hostnames the plugin may reach via hostApi.hostFetch. Dot-boundary suffix-match allow-list ('openai.azure.com' allows 'x.openai.azure.com' but not 'openai.azure.com.attacker.com'). Lowercase ASCII hostnames, >=1 dot, RFC1035 label/length bounds; wildcards / URLs / ports / whitespace / IDN-punycode (xn--*) / bare public suffixes are rejected — mirrors the host's load-time validator and the auth.partitionDomains rules so manifest-publish surfaces them before runtime. Max 16. The host re-validates at every hostFetch call.

reasoning.description: Human-readable justification surfaced to the user at install for the requested egress.

allowPrivateNetworks.description: Governance opt-in for allow-listed intranet/private-network egress. Applies to host-mediated hostApi.hostFetch and explicitly documented browser/direct intranet exceptions; loopback/link-local/metadata addresses remain denied by host guards where applicable.

hostSecrets.description: Explicit allowlist of host-managed LLM secrets the plugin reads via hostApi.getSecret. Default deny when unset.

read.description: Secret keys this plugin is permitted to read. Accepted forms: llm.apiKey.<vendor> or llm.marketplaceProvider.<presetId>.apiKey. Marketplace preset ids follow the host provider-preset id contract. Bounded (maxItems=32, per-item maxLength=111) to prevent registry-level DoS via oversized manifests.

startupTimeoutMs.description: Hard startup() timeout (ms). Exceeding plugins are fail-soft dropped. Default slow warning threshold is 5000ms.

auth.description: Optional declarative auth contract. Lets the host render a generic 'authentication required / signed in' badge + login/logout button in Settings → 플러그인 설정 without baking plugin-specific auth code into the host. The plugin still owns its OAuth/cookie flow (see architecture.md §9.4a 'Plugin-Owned OAuth Authentication'); the host only invokes the named tools and listens for the auth-changed event. Each of the tool names referenced here MUST be the `name` of an app-visible tool in `tools[]` (its `_meta.ui.visibility` includes `"app"`); the host value-validates this against the normalized Tool[]. On state transitions (login success, logout, token refresh failure) the plugin SHOULD emit an event named `<pluginId>.auth.changed` so the host UI can refresh without polling.

label.description: Human-readable label for the auth surface, shown next to the badge (e.g. 'Microsoft 계정', '사내 포털'). Defaults to the plugin's `name` if omitted.

statusTool.description: Name of an app-visible tool in `tools[]` (its `_meta.ui.visibility` includes `"app"`) returning `{ authenticated: boolean, account?: string }`. Host invokes on Settings tab mount and after every `<pluginId>.auth.changed` event.

loginTool.description: Name of an app-visible tool in `tools[]` (its `_meta.ui.visibility` includes `"app"`) the host invokes when the user clicks the '로그인' button. The plugin owns the actual auth flow (e.g. MSAL interactive, openAuthWindow); the host only triggers it.

logoutTool.description: Optional name of an app-visible tool in `tools[]` (its `_meta.ui.visibility` includes `"app"`) the host invokes when the user clicks the '로그아웃' button. Omit to render a sign-in-only surface (e.g. plugins that don't support runtime sign-out).

partitionDomains.description: Hostnames the plugin may open inside its `persist:plugin-auth:<pluginId>` partition via `hostApi.openAuthPartitionViewer`. Treated as a dot-boundary suffix-match allow-list (e.g. `outlook.office.com` matches `mail.outlook.office.com` but not `outlook.office.com.attacker.com`). Schema rules: lowercase ASCII hostnames only, each entry MUST contain ≥1 dot (single-label hosts like `localhost` are rejected to avoid blanket subdomain matching), per RFC 1035 each label is bounded to 63 chars and the whole hostname to 253. Wildcards / URLs / port suffixes / whitespace / NUL / IDN-punycode labels (`xn--*`) are rejected. The host's load-time validator additionally rejects bare public suffixes (`com`, `co.kr`, `or.kr`, `go.kr`, `kr`, `net`, `org`, `io`, `ai`, `dev`, `app`); those rejections are mirrored here so manifest-publish surfaces them before runtime. Length cap 16 prevents an over-broad consent surface. The host re-validates this list at every viewer-open call.

bypassFocusGate.description: When true, this event bypasses the host focus-gate AND cooldown-gate suppression and always fires an OS-level notification regardless of LVIS window focus state or recent prior emits from the same plugin. The host additionally promotes urgent=true for bypass events (sound + critical urgency) unless the caller explicitly opts out. Use for critical flows (meeting capture, security alerts) where the user must be notified out-of-band. Refs lvis-project/lvis-app#843.

dependencies.description: Plugin dependencies that should be installed or considered with this plugin. This is not a delivery mode.

agentApprovalScopes.description: §8 P0 security — approval action scopes this plugin is permitted to issue and respond to via hostApi.agentApproval. Omitted or empty array means no approval scopes allowed (host enforces empty-list semantics). Known values: 'agent_file_share', 'agent_task_delegate', 'agent_external_api_call'.

requires.description: Runtime prerequisites declared by the plugin

minAppVersion.description: Minimum LVIS app version (plain SemVer MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH, not a range) required to load this plugin. The host refuses to install (hard block before download) and refuses to activate (skips start(), surfaces a non-dismissable "needs newer app" state) when the running app is older. Omit for no minimum (compatible with all app versions).

llmKeySource.description: Whether this plugin uses host-managed LLM keys (host), its own keys (plugin), or no LLM at all (none). The 'host' source requires marketplace whitelist authorization at runtime.

packageName.description: npm package name persisted into installed manifests by PluginMarketplaceService for rollback support. Not authored by plugin developers.

icon.description: Optional Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. 'Mic', 'FileText'). Host dynamically looks up the named export from lucide-react and renders it as a monochrome stroke icon in the plugin grid popover. Unknown names fall back to the 'Plug' icon. Full list: https://lucide.dev/icons/

iconText.description: Optional short text (1-4 chars) rendered inside the plugin grid avatar — e.g. 'EP', 'MTG'. Takes precedence over `icon` when both are declared. Use when no Lucide glyph matches the plugin's domain identity.

python.description: Optional Python runtime co-deployment metadata. Host installs declared requirements when managedBy is lvis-app.

configSchema.description: §9.2 Track B — declarative settings schema. JSON Schema draft-07 subset rendered as a typed form in PluginConfigTab. format:'secret' routes the value through the encrypted keychain instead of cleartext pluginConfigs.

default.description: Default value for the form field. Outer JSON byte-size cap is enforced server-side (see marketplace publisher.py); the schema bounds object/array primitives so a pathological default can't blow up the catalog.

author.description: Plugin author — individual maintainer name or contact (distinct from `publisher`, which is the publishing organization). Surfaced in catalog / IDE intellisense; not used for permission decisions.

uiSlots.description: Top-level advertisement of UI slot names this plugin participates in. Marketplace metadata only — actual extension binding lives in `ui[].slot`. Kebab-case identifiers, ≤32 entries.

locales.description: Complete localized display copy and visible composer prefill. English is required as the deterministic fallback.

tool.$comment: PURE (v6) MCP Tool object — manifest == wire (#885). additionalProperties:false rejects the removed legacy fields (category/workerId/writesToOwnSandbox/version/deprecatedSince/replacedBy); they are not Tool properties.

mcpUiResourceCsp.$comment: MCP Apps spec `McpUiResourceCsp` — DOMAIN BUCKETS (not CSP directive names). Main derives the sandbox-proxy CSP header from these; the plugin never supplies a header string. Mirrors src/mcp/types.ts McpUiResourceCsp.

resourceDomains.description: Origins for images, scripts, stylesheets, fonts, media.

baseUriDomains.description: Allowed base URIs for the document -> base-uri.

mcpUiResourcePermissions.$comment: MCP Apps spec `McpUiResourcePermissions`, restricted to what this host can DEMONSTRABLY honor. Main derives the inner iframe's Permissions-Policy `allow` attribute AND the Electron session grant from this; the plugin never supplies either. An ABSENT key is denied. The inner frame carries `allow-same-origin` (spec origin requirement, apps.mdx:474-475), so it inherits the per-server proxy origin and a delegated feature can be honored. SOT for the accepted set: src/shared/mcp-app-permissions.ts MCP_APP_PERMISSION_FEATURES, proven end-to-end in test/e2e/ui/mcp-app-permissions.spec.ts. The spec's `clipboardWrite` is deliberately NOT accepted — measured, a script-initiated clipboard write is denied even when delegated — so declaring it FAILS validation loudly rather than silently doing nothing.

camera.description: Request the camera (getUserMedia video) for this card's frame. Absent means denied.

microphone.description: Request the microphone (getUserMedia audio) for this card's frame. Absent means denied.

geolocation.description: Request the Geolocation API for this card's frame. Absent means denied.
